BG 2.1#सञ्जय उवाच
तं तथा कृपयाविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम्
विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदन:#sañjaya uvācha
taṁ tathā kṛipayāviṣhṭamaśhru pūrṇākulekṣhaṇam
viṣhīdantamidaṁ vākyam uvācha madhusūdanaḥ#Sanjay said: Seeing Arjun overwhelmed with pity
column2: his mind grief-stricken
column3: and his eyes full of tears
column4: Shree Krishna spoke the following words.#sañjayaḥ uvācha—Sanjay said; tam—to him (Arjun); tathā—thus; kṛipayā—with pity; āviṣhṭam—overwhelmed; aśhru-pūrṇa—full of tears; ākula—distressed; īkṣhaṇam—eyes; viṣhīdantam—grief-stricken; idam—these; vākyam—words; uvācha—said; madhusūdanaḥ—Shree Krishn
column5: slayer of the Madhu demon

column1:
BG 2.2#श्रीभगवानुवाच
कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम्
अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
kutastvā kaśhmalamidaṁ viṣhame samupasthitam
anārya-juṣhṭamaswargyam akīrti-karam arjuna#The Supreme Lord said: My dear Arjun
column2: how has this delusion overcome you in this hour of peril? It is not befitting an honorable person. It leads not to the higher abodes
column3: but to disgrace.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Lord said; kutaḥ—wherefrom; tvā—to you; kaśhmalam—delusion; idam—this; viṣhame—in this hour of peril; samupasthitam—overcome; anārya—crude person; juṣhṭam—practiced; aswargyam—which does not lead to the higher abodes; akīrti-karam—leading to disgrace; arjuna—Arjun

column1: BG 2.3#क्लैब्यं मा स्म गम: पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते\nक्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप#klaibyaṁ mā sma gamaḥ pārtha naitat tvayyupapadyate\nkṣhudraṁ hṛidaya-daurbalyaṁ tyaktvottiṣhṭha parantapa#O Parth
column2: it does not befit you to yield to this unmanliness. Give up such petty weakness of heart and arise
column3: O vanquisher of enemies.#klaibyam—unmanliness; mā sma—do not; gamaḥ—yield to; pārtha—Arjun
column4: the son of Pritha; na—not; etat—this; tvayi—to you; upapadyate—befitting; kṣhudram—petty; hṛidaya—heart; daurbalyam—weakness; tyaktvā—giving up; uttiṣhṭha—arise; param-tapa—conqueror of enemies

column1:
BG 2.4#अर्जुन उवाच
कथं भीष्ममहं सङ्ख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन
इषुभि: प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन#arjuna uvācha
kathaṁ bhīṣhmam ahaṁ sankhye droṇaṁ cha madhusūdana
iṣhubhiḥ pratiyotsyāmi pūjārhāvari-sūdana#Arjun said: O Madhusudan
column2: how can I shoot arrows in battle on men like Bheeshma and Dronacharya
column3: who are worthy of my worship
column4: O destroyer of enemies?#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said; katham—how; bhīṣhmam—Bheeshma; aham—I; sankhye—in battle; droṇam—Dronacharya; cha—and; madhu-sūdana—Shree Krishn
column5: slayer of the Madhu demon; iṣhubhiḥ—with arrows; pratiyotsyāmi—shall I shoot; pūjā-arhau—worthy of worship; ari-sūdana—destroyer of enemies

column1:
BG 2.5#गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान्
श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके
हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैव
भुञ्जीय भोगान् रुधिरप्रदिग्धान्#gurūnahatvā hi mahānubhāvān
śhreyo bhoktuṁ bhaikṣhyamapīha loke
hatvārtha-kāmāṁstu gurūnihaiva
bhuñjīya bhogān rudhira-pradigdhān#It would be better to live in this world by begging
column2: than to enjoy life by killing these noble elders
column3: who are my teachers. If we kill them
column4: the wealth and pleasures we enjoy will be tainted with blood.#gurūn—teachers; ahatvā—not killing; hi—certainly; mahā-anubhāvān—noble elders; śhreyaḥ—better; bhoktum—to enjoy life; bhaikṣhyam—by begging; api—even; iha loke—in this world; hatvā—killing; artha—gain; kāmān—desiring; tu—but; gurūn—noble elders; iha—in this world; eva—certainly; bhuñjīya—enjoy; bhogān—pleasures; rudhira—blood; pradigdhān—tainted with

column1:
BG 2.6#न चैतद्विद्म: कतरन्नो गरीयो
यद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयु:
यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषाम
स्तेऽवस्थिता: प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्रा:#na chaitadvidmaḥ kataranno garīyo
yadvā jayema yadi vā no jayeyuḥ
yāneva hatvā na jijīviṣhāmas
te ’vasthitāḥ pramukhe dhārtarāṣhṭrāḥ#We do not even know which result of this war is preferable for us—conquering them or being conquered by them. Even after killing them we will not desire to live. Yet they have taken the side of the sons of Dhritarasthra
column2: and now stand before us on the battlefield.#na—not; cha—and; etat—this; vidmaḥ—we know; katarat—which; naḥ—for us; garīyaḥ—is preferable; yat vā—whether; jayema—we may conquer; yadi—if; vā—or; naḥ—us; jayeyuḥ—they may conquer; yān—whom; eva—certainly; hatvā—after killing; na—not; jijīviṣhāmaḥ—we desire to live; te—they; avasthitāḥ—are standing; pramukhe—before us; dhārtarāṣhṭrāḥ—the sons of Dhritarashtra

column1:
BG 2.7#कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभाव:
पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसम्मूढचेता:
यच्छ्रेय: स्यान्निश्चितं ब्रूहि तन्मे
शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम्#kārpaṇya-doṣhopahata-svabhāvaḥ
pṛichchhāmi tvāṁ dharma-sammūḍha-chetāḥ
yach-chhreyaḥ syānniśhchitaṁ brūhi tanme
śhiṣhyaste ’haṁ śhādhi māṁ tvāṁ prapannam#I am confused about my duty
column2: and am besieged with anxiety and faintheartedness. I am Your disciple
column3: and am surrendered to You. Please instruct me for certain what is best for me.#kārpaṇya-doṣha—the flaw of cowardice; upahata—besieged; sva-bhāvaḥ—nature; pṛichchhāmi—I am asking; tvām—to you; dharma—duty; sammūḍha—confused; chetāḥ—in heart; yat—what; śhreyaḥ—best; syāt—may be; niśhchitam—decisively; brūhi—tell; tat—that; me—to me; śhiṣhyaḥ—disciple; te—your; aham—I; śhādhi—please instruct; mām—me; tvām—unto you; prapannam—surrendered

column1:
BG 2.8#न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्याद्
यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम्
अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धं
राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम्#na hi prapaśhyāmi mamāpanudyād
yach-chhokam uchchhoṣhaṇam-indriyāṇām
avāpya bhūmāv-asapatnamṛiddhaṁ
rājyaṁ surāṇāmapi chādhipatyam#I can find no means of driving away this anguish that is drying up my senses. Even if I win a prosperous and unrivalled kingdom on the earth
column2: or gain sovereignty like the celestial gods
column3: I will be unable to dispel this grief.#na—not; hi—certainly; prapaśhyāmi—I see; mama—my; apanudyāt—drive away; yat—which; śhokam—anguish; uchchhoṣhaṇam—is drying up; indriyāṇām—of the senses; avāpya—after achieving; bhūmau—on the earth; asapatnam—unrivalled; ṛiddham—prosperous; rājyam—kingdom; surāṇām—like the celestial gods; api—even; cha—also; ādhipatyam—sovereignty

column1:
BG 2.9#सञ्जय उवाच
एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेश: परन्तप
न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह#sañjaya uvācha
evam-uktvā hṛiṣhīkeśhaṁ guḍākeśhaḥ parantapa
na yotsya iti govindam uktvā tūṣhṇīṁ babhūva ha#Sanjay said: Having thus spoken
column2: Gudakesh
column3: that chastiser of enemies
column4: addressed Hrishikesh: “Govind
column5: I shall not fight
column6: ” and became silent.#sañjayaḥ uvācha—Sanjay said; evam—thus; uktvā—having spoken; hṛiṣhīkeśham—to Shree Krishna
column7: the master of the mind and senses; guḍākeśhaḥ—Arjun
column9: the chastiser of the enemies; na yotsye—I shall not fight; iti—thus; govindam—Krishna
column10: the giver of pleasure to the senses; uktvā—having addressed; tūṣhṇīm—silent; babhūva—became ha

column1: BG 2.10#तमुवाच हृषीकेश: प्रहसन्निव भारत\nसेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वच:#tam-uvācha hṛiṣhīkeśhaḥ prahasanniva bhārata\nsenayorubhayor-madhye viṣhīdantam-idaṁ vachaḥ#O Dhritarashtra
column2: thereafter
column3: in the midst of both the armies
column4: Shree Krishna smilingly spoke the following words to the grief-stricken Arjun.#tam—to him; uvācha—said; hṛiṣhīkeśhaḥ—Shree Krishna
column5: Master of mind and senses; prahasan—smilingly; iva—as if; bhārata—Dhritarashtra
column6: descendant of Bharat; senayoḥ—of the armies; ubhayoḥ—of both; madhye—in the midst of; viṣhīdantam—to the grief-stricken; idam—this; vachaḥ—words

column1:
BG 2.11#श्रीभगवानुवाच
अशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे
गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिता:#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
aśhochyān-anvaśhochas-tvaṁ prajñā-vādānśh cha bhāṣhase
gatāsūn-agatāsūnśh-cha nānuśhochanti paṇḍitāḥ#The Supreme Lord said: While you speak words of wisdom
column2: you are mourning for that which is not worthy of grief. The wise lament neither for the living nor for the dead.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Lord said; aśhochyān—not worthy of grief; anvaśhochaḥ—are mourning; tvam—you; prajñā-vādān—words of wisdom; cha—and; bhāṣhase—speaking; gata āsūn—the dead; agata asūn—the living; cha—and; na—never; anuśhochanti—lament; paṇḍitāḥ—the wise

column1: BG 2.12#न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः\nन चैव न भविष्याम: सर्वे वयमत: परम्#na tvevāhaṁ jātu nāsaṁ na tvaṁ neme janādhipāḥ\nna chaiva na bhaviṣhyāmaḥ sarve vayamataḥ param#Never was there a time when I did not exist
column2: nor you
column3: nor all these kings; nor in the future shall any of us cease to be.#na—never; tu—however; eva—certainly; aham—I; jātu—at any time; na—nor; āsam—exist; na—nor; tvam—you; na—nor; ime—these; jana-adhipāḥ—kings; na—never; cha—also; eva—indeed; na bhaviṣhyāmaḥ—shall not exist; sarve vayam—all of us; ataḥ—from now; param—after

column1: BG 2.13#देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा\nतथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति#dehino ’smin yathā dehe kaumāraṁ yauvanaṁ jarā\ntathā dehāntara-prāptir dhīras tatra na muhyati#Just as the embodied soul continuously passes from childhood to youth to old age
column2: similarly
column3: at the time of death
column4: the soul passes into another body. The wise are not deluded by this.#dehinaḥ—of the embodied; asmin—in this; yathā—as; dehe—in the body; kaumāram—childhood; yauvanam—youth; jarā—old age; tathā—similarly; deha-antara—another body; prāptiḥ—achieves; dhīraḥ—the wise; tatra—thereupon; na muhyati—are not deluded

column1: BG 2.14#मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदु:खदा:\nआगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत#mātrā-sparśhās tu kaunteya śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ\nāgamāpāyino ’nityās tāns-titikṣhasva bhārata#O son of Kunti
column2: the contact between the senses and the sense objects gives rise to fleeting perceptions of happiness and distress. These are non-permanent
column3: and come and go like the winter and summer seasons. O descendent of Bharat
column4: one must learn to tolerate them without being disturbed.#mātrā-sparśhāḥ—contact of the senses with the sense objects; tu—indeed; kaunteya—Arjun
column5: the son of Kunti; śhīta—winter; uṣhṇa—summer; sukha—happiness; duḥkha—distress; dāḥ—give; āgama—come; apāyinaḥ—go; anityāḥ—non-permanent; tān—them; titikṣhasva—tolerate; bhārata—descendant of the Bharat

column1: BG 2.15#यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ\nसमदु:खसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते#yaṁ hi na vyathayantyete puruṣhaṁ puruṣharṣhabha\nsama-duḥkha-sukhaṁ dhīraṁ so ’mṛitatvāya kalpate#O Arjun
column2: noblest amongst men
column3: that person who is not affected by happiness and distress
column4: and remains steady in both
column5: becomes eligible for liberation.#yam—whom; hi—verily; na—not; vyathayanti—distressed; ete—these; puruṣham—person; puruṣha-ṛiṣhabha—the noblest amongst men
column6: Arjun; sama—equipoised; duḥkha—distress; sukham—happiness; dhīram—steady; saḥ—that person; amṛitatvāya—for liberation; kalpate—becomes eligible

column1: BG 2.16#नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सत:\nउभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभि:#nāsato vidyate bhāvo nābhāvo vidyate sataḥ\nubhayorapi dṛiṣhṭo ’nta stvanayos tattva-darśhibhiḥ#Of the transient there is no endurance
column2: and of the eternal there is no cessation. This has verily been observed and concluded by the seers of the Truth
column3: after studying the nature of both.#na—no; asataḥ—of the temporary; vidyate—there is; bhāvaḥ—is; na—no; abhāvaḥ—cessation; vidyate—is; sataḥ—of the eternal; ubhayoḥ—of the two; api—also; dṛiṣhṭaḥ—observed; antaḥ—conclusion; tu—verily; anayoḥ—of these; tattva—entity; darśhibhiḥ—by the seers

column1: BG 2.17#अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम्\nविनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित्कर्तुमर्हति#avināśhi tu tadviddhi yena sarvam idaṁ tatam\nvināśham avyayasyāsya na kaśhchit kartum arhati#That which pervades the entire body
column2: know it to be indestructible. No one can cause the destruction of the imperishable soul.#avināśhi—indestructible; tu—indeed; tat—that; viddhi—know; yena—by whom; sarvam—entire; idam—this; tatam—pervaded; vināśham—destruction; avyayasya—of the imperishable; asya—of it; na kaśhchit—no one; kartum—to cause; arhati—is able

column1: BG 2.18#अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ता: शरीरिण:\nअनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत#antavanta ime dehā nityasyoktāḥ śharīriṇaḥ\nanāśhino ’prameyasya tasmād yudhyasva bhārata#Only the material body is perishable; the embodied soul within is indestructible
column2: immeasurable
column3: and eternal. Therefore
column4: fight
column5: O descendent of Bharat.#anta-vantaḥ—having an end; ime—these; dehāḥ—material bodies; nityasya—eternally; uktāḥ—are said; śharīriṇaḥ—of the embodied soul; anāśhinaḥ—indestructible; aprameyasya—immeasurable; tasmāt—therefore; yudhyasva—fight; bhārata—descendant of Bharat

column1: BG 2.19#य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम्\nउभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते#ya enaṁ vetti hantāraṁ yaśh chainaṁ manyate hatam\nubhau tau na vijānīto nāyaṁ hanti na hanyate#Neither of them is in knowledge—the one who thinks the soul can slay and the one who thinks the soul can be slain. For truly
column2: the soul neither kills nor can it be killed.#yaḥ—one who; enam—this; vetti—knows; hantāram—the slayer; yaḥ—one who; cha—and; enam—this; manyate—thinks; hatam—slain; ubhau—both; tau—they; na—not; vijānītaḥ—in knowledge; na—neither; ayam—this; hanti—slays; na—nor; hanyate—is killed

column1:
BG 2.20#न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचि
नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूय:
अजो नित्य: शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो
न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे#na jāyate mriyate vā kadāchin
nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ
ajo nityaḥ śhāśhvato ’yaṁ purāṇo
na hanyate hanyamāne śharīre#The soul is neither born
column2: nor does it ever die; nor having once existed
column3: does it ever cease to be. The soul is without birth
column4: eternal
column5: immortal
column6: and ageless. It is not destroyed when the body is destroyed.#na jāyate—is not born; mriyate—dies; vā—or; kadāchit—at any time; na—not; ayam—this; bhūtvā—having once existed; bhavitā—will be; vā—or; na—not; bhūyaḥ—further; ajaḥ—unborn; nityaḥ—eternal; śhāśhvataḥ—immortal; ayam—this; purāṇaḥ—the ancient; na hanyate—is not destroyed; hanyamāne—is destroyed; śharīre—when the body

column1: BG 2.21#वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम्\nकथं स पुरुष: पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम्#vedāvināśhinaṁ nityaṁ ya enam ajam avyayam\nkathaṁ sa puruṣhaḥ pārtha kaṁ ghātayati hanti kam#O Parth
column2: how can one who knows the soul to be imperishable
column3: eternal
column4: unborn
column5: and immutable kill anyone or cause anyone to kill?#veda—knows; avināśhinam—imperishable; nityam—eternal; yaḥ—who; enam—this; ajam—unborn; avyayam—immutable; katham—how; saḥ—that; puruṣhaḥ—person; pārtha—Parth; kam—whom; ghātayati—causes to be killed; hanti—kills; kam—whom

column1:
BG 2.22#वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय
नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि
तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा
न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही#vāsānsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya
navāni gṛihṇāti naro ’parāṇi
tathā śharīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇānya
nyāni sanyāti navāni dehī#As a person sheds worn-out garments and wears new ones
column2: likewise
column3: at the time of death
column4: the soul casts off its worn-out body and enters a new one.#vāsānsi—garments; jīrṇāni—worn-out; yathā—as; vihāya—sheds; navāni—new; gṛihṇāti—accepts; naraḥ—a person; aparāṇi—others; tathā—likewise; śharīrāṇi—bodies; vihāya—casting off; jirṇāni—worn-out; anyāni—other; sanyāti—enters; navāni—new; dehī—the embodied soul

column1: BG 2.23#नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावक:\nन चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुत:#nainaṁ chhindanti śhastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥ\nna chainaṁ kledayantyāpo na śhoṣhayati mārutaḥ#Weapons cannot shred the soul
column2: nor can fire burn it. Water cannot wet it
column3: nor can the wind dry it.#na—not; enam—this soul; chhindanti—shred; śhastrāṇi—weapons; na—nor; enam—this soul; dahati—burns; pāvakaḥ—fire; na—not; cha—and; enam—this soul; kledayanti—moisten; āpaḥ—water; na—nor; śhoṣhayati—dry; mārutaḥ—wind

column1: BG 2.24#अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च\nनित्य: सर्वगत: स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातन:#achchhedyo ’yam adāhyo ’yam akledyo ’śhoṣhya eva cha\nnityaḥ sarva-gataḥ sthāṇur achalo ’yaṁ sanātanaḥ#The soul is unbreakable and incombustible; it can neither be dampened nor dried. It is everlasting
column2: in all places
column3: unalterable
column4: immutable
column5: and primordial.#achchhedyaḥ—unbreakable; ayam—this soul; adāhyaḥ—incombustible; ayam—this soul; akledyaḥ—cannot be dampened; aśhoṣhyaḥ—cannot be dried; eva—indeed; cha—and; nityaḥ—everlasting; sarva-gataḥ—all-pervading; sthāṇuḥ—unalterable; achalaḥ—immutable; ayam—this soul; sanātanaḥ—primordial

column1: BG 2.25#अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते\nतस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि#avyakto ’yam achintyo ’yam avikāryo ’yam uchyate\ntasmādevaṁ viditvainaṁ nānuśhochitum arhasi#The soul is spoken of as invisible
column2: inconceivable
column3: and unchangeable. Knowing this
column4: you should not grieve for the body.#avyaktaḥ—unmanifested; ayam—this soul; achintyaḥ—inconceivable; ayam—this soul; avikāryaḥ—unchangeable; ayam—this soul; uchyate—is said; tasmāt—therefore; evam—thus; viditvā—having known; enam—this soul; na—not; anuśhochitum—to grieve; arhasi—befitting

column1: BG 2.26#अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम्\nतथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैवं शोचितुमर्हसि#atha chainaṁ nitya-jātaṁ nityaṁ vā manyase mṛitam\ntathāpi tvaṁ mahā-bāho naivaṁ śhochitum arhasi#If
column2: however
column3: you think that the self is subject to constant birth and death
column4: O mighty-armed Arjun
column5: even then you should not grieve like this.#atha—if
column6: however; cha—and; enam—this soul; nitya-jātam—taking constant birth; nityam—always; vā—or; manyase—you think; mṛitam—dead; tathā api—even then; tvam—you; mahā-bāho—mighty-armed one
column7: Arjun; na—not; evam—like this; śhochitum—grieve; arhasi—befitting

column1: BG 2.27#जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च\nतस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि#jātasya hi dhruvo mṛityur dhruvaṁ janma mṛitasya cha\ntasmād aparihārye ’rthe na tvaṁ śhochitum arhasi#Death is certain for one who has been born
column2: and rebirth is inevitable for one who has died. Therefore
column3: you should not lament over the inevitable.#jātasya—for one who has been born; hi—for; dhruvaḥ—certain; mṛityuḥ—death; dhruvam—certain; janma—birth; mṛitasya—for the dead; cha—and; tasmāt—therefore; aparihārye arthe—in this inevitable situation; na—not; tvam—you; śhochitum—lament; arhasi—befitting

column1: BG 2.28#अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत\nअव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना#avyaktādīni bhūtāni vyakta-madhyāni bhārata\navyakta-nidhanānyeva tatra kā paridevanā#O scion of Bharat
column2: all created beings are unmanifest before birth
column3: manifest in life
column4: and again unmanifest on death. So why grieve?#avyakta-ādīni—unmanifest before birth; bhūtāni—created beings; vyakta—manifest; madhyāni—in the middle; bhārata—Arjun
column5: scion of Bharat; avyakta—unmanifest; nidhanāni—on death; eva—indeed; tatra—therefore; kā—why; paridevanā—grieve

column1:
BG 2.29#आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेन
माश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्य:
आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्य: शृ्णोति
श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित्#āśhcharya-vat paśhyati kaśhchid enan
āśhcharya-vad vadati tathaiva chānyaḥ
āśhcharya-vach chainam anyaḥ śhṛiṇoti
śhrutvāpyenaṁ veda na chaiva kaśhchit#Some see the soul as amazing
column2: some describe it as amazing
column3: and some hear of the soul as amazing
column4: while others
column5: even on hearing
column6: cannot understand it at all.#āśhcharya-vat—as amazing; paśhyati—see; kaśhchit—someone; enam—this soul; āśhcharya-vat—as amazing; vadati—speak of; tathā—thus; eva—indeed; cha—and; anyaḥ—other; āśhcharya-vat—similarly amazing; cha—also; enam—this soul; anyaḥ—others; śhṛiṇoti—hear; śhrutvā—having heard; api—even; enam—this soul; veda—understand; na—not; cha—and; eva—even; kaśhchit—some

column1: BG 2.30#देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत\nतस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि#dehī nityam avadhyo ’yaṁ dehe sarvasya bhārata\ntasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni na tvaṁ śhochitum arhasi#O Arjun
column2: the soul that dwells within the body is immortal; therefore
column3: you should not mourn for anyone.#dehī—the soul that dwells within the body; nityam—always; avadhyaḥ—immortal; ayam—this soul; dehe—in the body; sarvasya—of everyone; bhārata—descendant of Bharat
column4: Arjun; tasmāt—therefore; sarvāṇi—for all; bhūtāni—living entities; na—not; tvam—you; śhochitum—mourn; arhasi—should

column1: BG 2.31#स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि\nधर्म्याद्धि युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते#swa-dharmam api chāvekṣhya na vikampitum arhasi\ndharmyāddhi yuddhāch chhreyo ’nyat kṣhatriyasya na vidyate#Besides
column2: considering your duty as a warrior
column3: you should not waver. Indeed
column4: for a warrior
column5: there is no better engagement than fighting for upholding of righteousness.#swa-dharmam—one’s duty in accordance with the Vedas; api—also; cha—and; avekṣhya—considering; na—not; vikampitum—to waver; arhasi—should; dharmyāt—for righteousness; hi—indeed; yuddhāt—than fighting; śhreyaḥ—better; anyat—another; kṣhatriyasya—of a warrior; na—not; vidyate—exists

column1: BG 2.32#यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम्\nसुखिन: क्षत्रिया: पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम्#yadṛichchhayā chopapannaṁ swarga-dvāram apāvṛitam\nsukhinaḥ kṣhatriyāḥ pārtha labhante yuddham īdṛiśham#O Parth
column2: happy are the warriors to whom such opportunities to defend righteousness come unsought
column3: opening for them the stairway to the celestial abodes.#yadṛichchhayā—unsought; cha—and; upapannam—come; swarga—celestial abodes; dvāram—door; apāvṛitam—wide open; sukhinaḥ—happy; kṣhatriyāḥ—warriors; pārtha—Arjun
column4: the son of Pritha; labhante—obtain; yuddham—war; īdṛiśham—such

column1: BG 2.33#अथ चेतत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं संग्रामं न करिष्यसि\nतत: स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि#atha chet tvam imaṁ dharmyaṁ saṅgrāmaṁ na kariṣhyasi\ntataḥ sva-dharmaṁ kīrtiṁ cha hitvā pāpam avāpsyasi#If
column2: however
column3: you refuse to fight this righteous war
column4: abandoning your social duty and reputation
column5: you will certainly incur sin.#atha chet—if
column6: however; tvam—you; imam—this; dharmyam saṅgrāmam—righteous war; na—not; kariṣhyasi—act; tataḥ—then; sva-dharmam—one’s duty in accordance with the Vedas; kīrtim—reputation; cha—and; hitvā—abandoning; pāpam—sin; avāpsyasi—will incur

column1: BG 2.34#अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम्\nसम्भावितस्य चाकीर्ति र्मरणादतिरिच्यते#akīrtiṁ chāpi bhūtāni kathayiṣhyanti te ’vyayām\nsambhāvitasya chākīrtir maraṇād atirichyate#People will speak of you as a coward and a deserter. For a respectable person
column2: infamy is worse than death.#akīrtim—infamy; cha—and; api—also; bhūtāni—people; kathayiṣhyanti—will speak; te—of your; avyayām—everlasting; sambhāvitasya—of a respectable person; cha—and; akīrtiḥ—infamy; maraṇāt—than death; atirichyate—is greater

column1: BG 2.35#भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथा:\nयेषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम्#bhayād raṇād uparataṁ mansyante tvāṁ mahā-rathāḥ\nyeṣhāṁ cha tvaṁ bahu-mato bhūtvā yāsyasi lāghavam#The great generals who hold you in high esteem will think that you fled from the battlefield out of fear
column2: and thus will lose their respect for you.#bhayāt—out of fear; raṇāt—from the battlefield; uparatam—have fled; mansyante—will think; tvām—you; mahā-rathāḥ—warriors who could single handedly match the strength of ten thousand ordinary warriors; yeṣhām—for whom; cha—and; tvam—you; bahu-mataḥ—highly-esteemed; bhūtvā—having been; yāsyasi—you will lose; lāghavam—decreased in value

column1: BG 2.36#अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून्वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिता:\nनिन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दु:खतरं नु किम्#avāchya-vādānśh cha bahūn vadiṣhyanti tavāhitāḥ\nnindantastava sāmarthyaṁ tato duḥkhataraṁ nu kim#Your enemies will defame and humiliate you with unkind words
column2: disparaging your might. Alas
column3: what could be more painful than that?#avāchya-vādān—using harsh words; cha—and; bahūn—many; vadiṣhyanti—will say; tava—your; ahitāḥ—enemies; nindantaḥ—defame; tava—your; sāmarthyam—might; tataḥ—than that; duḥkha-taram—more painful; nu—indeed; kim—what

column1: BG 2.37#हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम्\nतस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चय:#hato vā prāpsyasi swargaṁ jitvā vā bhokṣhyase mahīm\ntasmād uttiṣhṭha kaunteya yuddhāya kṛita-niśhchayaḥ#If you fight
column2: you will either be slain on the battlefield and go to the celestial abodes
column3: or you will gain victory and enjoy the kingdom on earth. Therefore arise with determination
column4: O son of Kunti
column5: and be prepared to fight.#hataḥ—slain; vā—or; prāpsyasi—you will attain; swargam—celestial abodes; jitvā—by achieving victory; vā—or; bhokṣhyase—you shall enjoy; mahīm—the kingdom on earth; tasmāt—therefore; uttiṣhṭha—arise; kaunteya—Arjun
column6: the son of Kunti; yuddhāya—for fight; kṛita-niśhchayaḥ—with determination

column1: BG 2.38#सुखदु:खे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ\nततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि#sukha-duḥkhe same kṛitvā lābhālābhau jayājayau\ntato yuddhāya yujyasva naivaṁ pāpam avāpsyasi#Fight for the sake of duty
column2: treating alike happiness and distress
column3: loss and gain
column4: victory and defeat. Fulfilling your responsibility in this way
column5: you will never incur sin.#sukha—happiness; duḥkhe—in distress; same kṛitvā—treating alike; lābha-alābhau—gain and loss; jaya-ajayau—victory and defeat; tataḥ—thereafter; yuddhāya—for fighting; yujyasva—engage; na—never; evam—thus; pāpam—sin; avāpsyasi—shall incur

column1: BG 2.39#एषा तेऽभिहिता साङ्ख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां शृणु\nबुद्ध्या युक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि#eṣhā te ’bhihitā sānkhye buddhir yoge tvimāṁ śhṛiṇu\nbuddhyā yukto yayā pārtha karma-bandhaṁ prahāsyasi#Hitherto
column2: I have explained to you Sānkhya Yog
column3: or analytic knowledge regarding the nature of the soul. Now listen
column4: O Parth
column5: as I reveal Buddhi Yog
column6: or the Yog of Intellect. When you work with such understanding
column7: you will be freed from the bondage of karma.#eṣhā—hitherto; te—to you; abhihitā—explained; sānkhye—by analytical knowledge; buddhiḥ yoge—by the yog of intellect; tu—indeed; imām—this; śhṛiṇu—listen; buddhyā—by understanding; yuktaḥ—united; yayā—by which; pārtha—Arjun
column8: the son of Pritha; karma-bandham—bondage of karma; prahāsyasi—you shall be released from

column1: BG 2.40#नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते\nस्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात्#nehābhikrama-nāśho ’sti pratyavāyo na vidyate\nsvalpam apyasya dharmasya trāyate mahato bhayāt#Working in this state of consciousness
column2: there is no loss or adverse result
column3: and even a little effort saves one from great danger.#na—not; iha—in this; abhikrama—efforts; nāśhaḥ—loss; asti—there is; pratyavāyaḥ—adverse result; na—not; vidyate—is; su-alpam—a little; api—even; asya—of this; dharmasya—occupation; trāyate—saves; mahataḥ—from great; bhayāt—danger

column1: BG 2.41#व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन\nबहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम्#vyavasāyātmikā buddhir ekeha kuru-nandana\nbahu-śhākhā hyanantāśh cha buddhayo ’vyavasāyinām#O descendent of the Kurus
column2: the intellect of those who are on this path is resolute
column3: and their aim is one-pointed. But the intellect of those who are irresolute is many-branched.#vyavasāya-ātmikā—resolute; buddhiḥ—intellect; ekā—single; iha—on this path; kuru-nandana—descendent of the Kurus; bahu-śhākhāḥ—many-branched; hi—indeed; anantāḥ—endless; cha—also; buddhayaḥ—intellect; avyavasāyinām—of the irresolute

column1:
BG 2.42#यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चित:
वेदवादरता: पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिन:
कामात्मान: स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम्
क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति#yāmimāṁ puṣhpitāṁ vāchaṁ pravadanty-avipaśhchitaḥ
veda-vāda-ratāḥ pārtha nānyad astīti vādinaḥ
kāmātmānaḥ swarga-parā janma-karma-phala-pradām
kriyā-viśheṣha-bahulāṁ bhogaiśhwarya-gatiṁ prati#BG 2.42-43:#yām imām—all these; puṣhpitām—flowery; vācham—words; pravadanti—speak; avipaśhchitaḥ—those with limited understanding; veda-vāda-ratāḥ—attached to the flowery words of the Vedas; pārtha—Arjun
column2: the son of Pritha; na anyat—no other; asti—is; iti—thus; vādinaḥ—advocate; kāma-ātmānaḥ—desirous of sensual pleasure; swarga-parāḥ—aiming to achieve the heavenly planets; janma-karma-phala—high birth and fruitive results; pradāṁ—awarding; kriyā-viśheṣha—pompous ritualistic ceremonies; bahulām—various; bhoga—gratification; aiśhwarya—luxury; gatim—progress; prati—toward

column1:
BG 2.43#यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चित:
वेदवादरता: पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिन:
कामात्मान: स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम्
क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति#yāmimāṁ puṣhpitāṁ vāchaṁ pravadanty-avipaśhchitaḥ
veda-vāda-ratāḥ pārtha nānyad astīti vādinaḥ
kāmātmānaḥ swarga-parā janma-karma-phala-pradām
kriyā-viśheṣha-bahulāṁ bhogaiśhwarya-gatiṁ prati#BG 2.42-43:#yām imām—all these; puṣhpitām—flowery; vācham—words; pravadanti—speak; avipaśhchitaḥ—those with limited understanding; veda-vāda-ratāḥ—attached to the flowery words of the Vedas; pārtha—Arjun
column2: the son of Pritha; na anyat—no other; asti—is; iti—thus; vādinaḥ—advocate; kāma-ātmānaḥ—desirous of sensual pleasure; swarga-parāḥ—aiming to achieve the heavenly planets; janma-karma-phala—high birth and fruitive results; pradāṁ—awarding; kriyā-viśheṣha—pompous ritualistic ceremonies; bahulām—various; bhoga—gratification; aiśhwarya—luxury; gatim—progress; prati—toward

column1: BG 2.44#भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम्\nव्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धि: समाधौ न विधीयते#bhogaiśwvarya-prasaktānāṁ tayāpahṛita-chetasām\nvyavasāyātmikā buddhiḥ samādhau na vidhīyate#With their minds deeply attached to worldly pleasures and their intellects bewildered by such things
column2: they are unable to possess the resolute determination for success on the path to God.#bhoga—gratification; aiśhwarya—luxury; prasaktānām—whose minds are deeply attached; tayā—by that; apahṛita-chetasām—bewildered in intellect; vyavasāya-ātmikā—resolute; buddhiḥ—intellect; samādhau—fulfilment; na—never; vidhīyate—occurs

column1: BG 2.45#त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन\nनिर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान्#trai-guṇya-viṣhayā vedā nistrai-guṇyo bhavārjuna\nnirdvandvo nitya-sattva-stho niryoga-kṣhema ātmavān#The Vedas deal with the three modes of material nature
column2: O Arjun. Rise above the three modes to a state of pure spiritual consciousness. Freeing yourself from dualities
column3: eternally fixed in Truth
column4: and without concern for material gain and safety
column5: be situated in the self.#trai-guṇya—of the three modes of material nature; viṣhayāḥ—subject matter; vedāḥ—Vedic scriptures; nistrai-guṇyaḥ—above the three modes of material nature
column6: transcendental; bhava—be; arjuna—Arjun; nirdvandvaḥ—free from dualities; nitya-sattva-sthaḥ—eternally fixed in truth; niryoga-kṣhemaḥ—unconcerned about gain and preservation; ātma-vān—situated in the self

column1: BG 2.46#यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वत: सम्प्लुतोदके\nतावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानत:#yāvān artha udapāne sarvataḥ samplutodake\ntāvānsarveṣhu vedeṣhu brāhmaṇasya vijānataḥ#Whatever purpose is served by a small well of water is naturally served in all respects by a large lake. Similarly
column2: one who realizes the Absolute Truth also fulfills the purpose of all the Vedas.#yāvān—whatever; arthaḥ—purpose; uda-pāne—a well of water; sarvataḥ—in all respects; sampluta-udake—by a large lake; tāvān—that many; sarveṣhu—in all; vedeṣhu—Vedas; brāhmaṇasya—one who realizes the Absolute Truth; vijānataḥ—who is in complete knowledge

column1: BG 2.47#कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन\nमा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि#karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣhu kadāchana\nmā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stvakarmaṇi#You have a right to perform your prescribed duties
column2: but you are not entitled to the fruits of your actions. Never consider yourself to be the cause of the results of your activities
column3: nor be attached to inaction.#karmaṇi—in prescribed duties; eva—only; adhikāraḥ—right; te—your; mā—not; phaleṣhu—in the fruits; kadāchana—at any time; mā—never; karma-phala—results of the activities; hetuḥ—cause; bhūḥ—be; mā—not; te—your; saṅgaḥ—attachment; astu—must be; akarmaṇi—in inaction

column1: BG 2.48#योगस्थ: कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय\nसिद्ध्यसिद्ध्यो: समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते#yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañjaya\nsiddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga uchyate#Be steadfast in the performance of your duty
column2: O Arjun
column3: abandoning attachment to success and failure. Such equanimity is called Yog.#yoga-sthaḥ—being steadfast in yog; kuru—perform; karmāṇi—duties; saṅgam—attachment; tyaktvā—having abandoned; dhanañjaya—Arjun; siddhi-asiddhyoḥ—in success and failure; samaḥ—equipoised; bhūtvā—becoming; samatvam—equanimity; yogaḥ—Yog; uchyate—is called

column1: BG 2.49#दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय\nबुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणा: फलहेतव:#dūreṇa hy-avaraṁ karma buddhi-yogād dhanañjaya\nbuddhau śharaṇam anvichchha kṛipaṇāḥ phala-hetavaḥ#Seek refuge in divine knowledge and insight
column2: O Arjun
column3: and discard reward-seeking actions that are certainly inferior to works performed with the intellect established in divine knowledge. Miserly are those who seek to enjoy the fruits of their works.#dūreṇa—(discrad) from far away; hi—certainly; avaram—inferior; karma—reward-seeking actions; buddhi-yogāt—with the intellect established in divine knowledge; dhanañjaya—Arjun; buddhau—divine knowledge and insight; śharaṇam—refuge; anvichchha—seek; kṛipaṇāḥ—miserly; phala-hetavaḥ—those seeking fruits of their work

column1: BG 2.50#बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते\nतस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योग: कर्मसु कौशलम्#buddhi-yukto jahātīha ubhe sukṛita-duṣhkṛite\ntasmād yogāya yujyasva yogaḥ karmasu kauśhalam#One who prudently practices the science of work without attachment can get rid of both good and bad reactions in this life itself. Therefore
column2: strive for Yog
column3: which is the art of working skillfully (in proper consciousness).#buddhi-yuktaḥ—endowed with wisdom; jahāti—get rid of; iha—in this life; ubhe—both; sukṛita-duṣhkṛite—good and bad deeds; tasmāt—therefore; yogāya—for Yog; yujyasva—strive for; yogaḥ—yog is; karmasu kauśhalam—the art of working skillfully

column1: BG 2.51#कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिण:\nजन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ता: पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम्#karma-jaṁ buddhi-yuktā hi phalaṁ tyaktvā manīṣhiṇaḥ\njanma-bandha-vinirmuktāḥ padaṁ gachchhanty-anāmayam#The wise endowed with equanimity of intellect
column2: abandon attachment to the fruits of actions
column3: which bind one to the cycle of life and death. By working in such consciousness
column4: they attain the state beyond all suffering.#karma-jam—born of fruitive actions; buddhi-yuktāḥ—endowed with equanimity of intellect; hi—as; phalam—fruits; tyaktvā—abandoning; manīṣhiṇaḥ—the wise; janma-bandha-vinirmuktāḥ—freedom from the bondage of life and death; padam—state; gachchhanti—attain; anāmayam—devoid of sufferings

column1: BG 2.52#यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति\nतदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च#yadā te moha-kalilaṁ buddhir vyatitariṣhyati\ntadā gantāsi nirvedaṁ śhrotavyasya śhrutasya cha#When your intellect crosses the quagmire of delusion
column2: you will then acquire indifference to what has been heard and what is yet to be heard (about enjoyments in this world and the next).#yadā—when; te—your; moha—delusion; kalilam—quagmire; buddhiḥ—intellect; vyatitariṣhyati—crosses; tadā—then; gantāsi—you shall acquire; nirvedam—indifferent; śhrotavyasya—to what is yet to be heard; śhrutasya—to what has been heard; cha—and

column1: BG 2.53#श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला\nसमाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि#śhruti-vipratipannā te yadā sthāsyati niśhchalā\nsamādhāv-achalā buddhis tadā yogam avāpsyasi#When your intellect ceases to be allured by the fruitive sections of the Vedas and remains steadfast in divine consciousness
column2: you will then attain the state of perfect Yog.#śhruti-vipratipannā—not allured by the fruitive sections of the Vedas; te—your; yadā—when; sthāsyati—remains; niśhchalā—steadfast; samādhau—in divine consciousness; achalā—steadfast; buddhiḥ—intellect; tadā—at that time; yogam—Yog; avāpsyasi—you will attain

column1:
BG 2.54#अर्जुन उवाच
स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव
स्थितधी: किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम्#arjuna uvācha
sthita-prajñasya kā bhāṣhā samādhi-sthasya keśhava
sthita-dhīḥ kiṁ prabhāṣheta kim āsīta vrajeta kim#Arjun said : O Keshav
column2: what is the disposition of one who is situated in divine consciousness? How does an enlightened person talk? How does he sit? How does he walk?#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said; sthita-prajñasya—one with steady intellect; kā—what; bhāṣhā—talk; samādhi-sthasya—situated in divine consciousness; keśhava—Shree Krishna
column3: killer of the Keshi Demon; sthita-dhīḥ—enlightened person; kim—what; prabhāṣheta—talks; kim—how; āsīta—sits; vrajeta—walks; kim—how

column1:
BG 2.55#श्रीभगवानुवाच
प्रजहाति यदा कामान्सर्वान्पार्थ मनोगतान्
आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्ट: स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
prajahāti yadā kāmān sarvān pārtha mano-gatān
ātmany-evātmanā tuṣhṭaḥ sthita-prajñas tadochyate#The Supreme Lord said: O Parth
column2: when one discards all selfish desires and cravings of the senses that torment the mind
column3: and becomes satisfied in the realization of the self
column4: such a person is said to be transcendentally situated.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—The Supreme Lord said; prajahāti—discards; yadā—when; kāmān—selfish desires; sarvān—all; pārtha—Arjun
column5: the son of Pritha; manaḥ-gatān—of the mind; ātmani—of the self; eva—only; ātmanā—by the purified mind; tuṣhṭaḥ—satisfied; sthita-prajñaḥ—one with steady intellect; tadā—at that time; uchyate—is said

column1: BG 2.56#दु:खेष्वनुद्विग्नमना: सुखेषु विगतस्पृह:\nवीतरागभयक्रोध: स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते#duḥkheṣhv-anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣhu vigata-spṛihaḥ\nvīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhaḥ sthita-dhīr munir uchyate#One whose mind remains undisturbed amidst misery
column2: who does not crave for pleasure
column3: and who is free from attachment
column4: fear
column5: and anger
column6: is called a sage of steady wisdom.#duḥkheṣhu—amidst miseries; anudvigna-manāḥ—one whose mind is undisturbed; sukheṣhu—in pleasure; vigata-spṛihaḥ—without craving; vīta—free from; rāga—attachment; bhaya—fear; krodhaḥ—anger; sthita-dhīḥ—enlightened person; muniḥ—a sage; uchyate—is called

column1: BG 2.57#य: सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम्\nनाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता#yaḥ sarvatrānabhisnehas tat tat prāpya śhubhāśhubham\nnābhinandati na dveṣhṭi tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā#One who remains unattached under all conditions
column2: and is neither delighted by good fortune nor dejected by tribulation
column3: he is a sage with perfect knowledge.#yaḥ—who; sarvatra—in all conditions; anabhisnehaḥ—unattached; tat—that; tat—that; prāpya—attaining; śhubha—good; aśhubham—evil; na—neither; abhinandati—delight in; na—nor; dveṣhṭi—dejected by; tasya—his; prajñā—knowledge; pratiṣhṭhitā—is fixed

column1: BG 2.58#यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वश:\nइन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता#yadā sanharate chāyaṁ kūrmo ’ṅgānīva sarvaśhaḥ\nindriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā#One who is able to withdraw the senses from their objects
column2: just as a tortoise withdraws its limbs into its shell
column3: is established in divine wisdom.#yadā—when; sanharate—withdraw; cha—and; ayam—this; kūrmaḥ—tortoise; aṅgāni—limbs; iva—as; sarvaśhaḥ—fully; indriyāṇi—senses; indriya-arthebhyaḥ—from the sense objects; tasya—his; prajñā—divine wisdom; pratiṣhṭhitā—fixed in

column1: BG 2.59#विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिन:\nरसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते#viṣhayā vinivartante nirāhārasya dehinaḥ\nrasa-varjaṁ raso ’pyasya paraṁ dṛiṣhṭvā nivartate#Aspirants may restrain the senses from their objects of enjoyment
column2: but the taste for the sense objects remains. However
column3: even this taste ceases for those who realize the Supreme.#viṣhayāḥ—objects for senses; vinivartante—restrain; nirāhārasya—practicing self restraint; dehinaḥ—for the embodied; rasa-varjam—cessation of taste; rasaḥ—taste; api—however; asya—person’s; param—the Supreme; dṛiṣhṭvā—on realization; nivartate—ceases to be

column1: BG 2.60#यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चित:\nइन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मन:#yatato hyapi kaunteya puruṣhasya vipaśhchitaḥ\nindriyāṇi pramāthīni haranti prasabhaṁ manaḥ#The senses are so strong and turbulent
column2: O son of Kunti
column3: that they can forcibly carry away the mind even of a person endowed with discrimination who practices self-control.#yatataḥ—while practicing self-control; hi—for; api—even; kaunteya—Arjun
column4: the son of Kunti; puruṣhasya—of a person; vipaśhchitaḥ—one endowed with discrimination; indriyāṇi—the senses; pramāthīni—turbulent; haranti—carry away; prasabham—forcibly; manaḥ—the mind

column1: BG 2.61#तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्पर:\nवशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता#tāni sarvāṇi sanyamya yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ\nvaśhe hi yasyendriyāṇi tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā#They are established in perfect knowledge
column2: who subdue their senses and keep their minds ever absorbed in Me.#tāni—them; sarvāṇi—all; sanyamya—subduing; yuktaḥ—united; āsīta—seated; mat-paraḥ—toward me (Shree Krishna); vaśhe—control; hi—certainly; yasya—whose; indriyāṇi—senses; tasya—their; prajñā—perfect knowledge; pratiṣhṭhitā—is fixed;

column1: BG 2.62#ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंस: सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते\nसङ्गात्सञ्जायते काम: कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते#dhyāyato viṣhayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣhūpajāyate\nsaṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate#While contemplating on the objects of the senses
column2: one develops attachment to them. Attachment leads to desire
column3: and from desire arises anger.#dhyāyataḥ—contemplating; viṣhayān—sense objects; puṁsaḥ—of a person; saṅgaḥ—attachment; teṣhu—to them (sense objects); upajāyate—arises; saṅgāt—from attachment; sañjāyate—develops; kāmaḥ—desire; kāmāt—from desire; krodhaḥ—anger; abhijāyate—arises

column1: BG 2.63#क्रोधाद्भवति सम्मोह: सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रम:\nस्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति#krodhād bhavati sammohaḥ sammohāt smṛiti-vibhramaḥ\nsmṛiti-bhranśhād buddhi-nāśho buddhi-nāśhāt praṇaśhyati#Anger leads to clouding of judgment
column2: which results in bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered
column3: the intellect gets destroyed; and when the intellect is destroyed
column4: one is ruined.#krodhāt—from anger; bhavati—comes; sammohaḥ—clouding of judgement; sammohāt—from clouding of judgement; smṛiti—memory; vibhramaḥ—bewilderment; smṛiti-bhranśhāt—from bewilderment of memory; buddhi-nāśhaḥ—destruction of intellect; buddhi-nāśhāt—from destruction of intellect; praṇaśhyati—one is ruined

column1: BG 2.64#रागद्वेषवियुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश्चरन्\nआत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति#rāga-dveṣha-viyuktais tu viṣhayān indriyaiśh charan\nātma-vaśhyair-vidheyātmā prasādam adhigachchhati#But one who controls the mind
column2: and is free from attachment and aversion
column3: even while using the objects of the senses
column4: attains the Grace of God.#rāga—attachment; dveṣha—aversion; viyuktaiḥ—free; tu—but; viṣhayān—objects of the senses; indriyaiḥ—by the senses; charan—while using; ātma-vaśhyaiḥ—controlling one’s mind; vidheya-ātmā—one who controls the mind; prasādam—grace of God; adhigachchhati—attains

column1: BG 2.65#प्रसादे सर्वदु:खानां हानिरस्योपजायते\nप्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धि: पर्यवतिष्ठते#prasāde sarva-duḥkhānāṁ hānir asyopajāyate\nprasanna-chetaso hyāśhu buddhiḥ paryavatiṣhṭhate#By divine grace comes the peace in which all sorrows end
column2: and the intellect of such a person of tranquil mind soon becomes firmly established in God.#prasāde—by divine grace; sarva—all; duḥkhānām—sorrows; hāniḥ—destruction; asya—his; upajāyate—comes; prasanna-chetasaḥ—with a tranquil mind; hi—indeed; āśhu—soon; buddhiḥ—intellect; paryavatiṣhṭhate—becomes firmly established

column1: BG 2.66#नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना\nन चाभावयत: शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुत: सुखम्#nāsti buddhir-ayuktasya na chāyuktasya bhāvanā\nna chābhāvayataḥ śhāntir aśhāntasya kutaḥ sukham#But an undisciplined person
column2: who has not controlled the mind and senses
column3: can neither have a resolute intellect nor steady contemplation on God. For one who never unites the mind with God there is no peace; and how can one who lacks peace be happy?#na—not; asti—is; buddhiḥ—intellect; ayuktasya—not united; na—not; cha—and; ayuktasya—not united; bhāvanā—contemplation; na—nor; cha—and; abhāvayataḥ—for those not united; śhāntiḥ—peace; aśhāntasya—of the unpeaceful; kutaḥ—where; sukham—happiness

column1: BG 2.67#इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते\nतदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि#indriyāṇāṁ hi charatāṁ yan mano ’nuvidhīyate\ntadasya harati prajñāṁ vāyur nāvam ivāmbhasi#Just as a strong wind sweeps a boat off its chartered course on the water
column2: even one of the senses on which the mind focuses can lead the intellect astray.#indriyāṇām—of the senses; hi—indeed; charatām—roaming; yat—which; manaḥ—the mind; anuvidhīyate—becomes constantly engaged; tat—that; asya—of that; harati—carries away; prajñām—intellect; vāyuḥ—wind; nāvam—boat; iva—as; ambhasi—on the water

column1: BG 2.68#तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वश:\nइन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता#tasmād yasya mahā-bāho nigṛihītāni sarvaśhaḥ\nindriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā#Therefore
column2: one who has restrained the senses from their objects
column3: O mighty armed Arjun
column4: is firmly established in transcendental knowledge.#tasmāt—therefore; yasya—whose; mahā-bāho—mighty-armed one; nigṛihītāni—restrained; sarvaśhaḥ—completely; indriyāṇi—senses; indriya-arthebhyaḥ—from sense objects; tasya—of that person; prajñā—transcendental knowledge; pratiṣhṭhitā—remains fixed

column1: BG 2.69#या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी\nयस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुने:#yā niśhā sarva-bhūtānāṁ tasyāṁ jāgarti sanyamī\nyasyāṁ jāgrati bhūtāni sā niśhā paśhyato muneḥ#What all beings consider as day is the night of ignorance for the wise
column2: and what all creatures see as night is the day for the introspective sage.#yā—which; niśhā—night; sarva-bhūtānām—of all living beings; tasyām—in that; jāgarti—awake; sanyamī—self-controlled; yasyām—in which; jāgrati—awake; bhūtāni—creatures; sā—that; niśhā—night; paśhyataḥ—see; muneḥ—sage

column1:
BG 2.70#आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं
समुद्रमाप: प्रविशन्ति यद्वत्
तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे
स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी#āpūryamāṇam achala-pratiṣhṭhaṁ
samudram āpaḥ praviśhanti yadvat
tadvat kāmā yaṁ praviśhanti sarve
sa śhāntim āpnoti na kāma-kāmī#Just as the ocean remains undisturbed by the incessant flow of waters from rivers merging into it
column2: likewise the sage who is unmoved despite the flow of desirable objects all around him attains peace
column3: and not the person who strives to satisfy desires.#āpūryamāṇam—filled from all sides; achala-pratiṣhṭham—undisturbed; samudram—ocean; āpaḥ—waters; praviśhanti—enter; yadvat—as; tadvat—likewise; kāmāḥ—desires; yam—whom; praviśhanti—enter; sarve—all; saḥ—that person; śhāntim—peace; āpnoti—attains; na—not; kāma-kāmī—one who strives to satisfy desires

column1: BG 2.71#विहाय कामान्य: सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति नि:स्पृह:\nनिर्ममो निरहङ्कार: स शान्तिमधिगच्छति#vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān pumānśh charati niḥspṛihaḥ\nnirmamo nirahankāraḥ sa śhāntim adhigachchhati#That person
column2: who gives up all material desires and lives free from a sense of greed
column3: proprietorship
column4: and egoism
column5: attains perfect peace.#vihāya—giving up; kāmān—material desires; yaḥ—who; sarvān—all; pumān—a person; charati—lives; niḥspṛihaḥ—free from hankering; nirmamaḥ—without a sense of proprietorship; nirahankāraḥ—without ego; saḥ—that person; śhāntim—perfect peace; adhigachchhati—attains

column1: BG 2.72#एषा ब्राह्मी स्थिति: पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति\nस्थित्वास्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति#eṣhā brāhmī sthitiḥ pārtha naināṁ prāpya vimuhyati\nsthitvāsyām anta-kāle ’pi brahma-nirvāṇam ṛichchhati#O Parth
column2: such is the state of an enlightened soul that having attained it
column3: one is never again deluded. Being established in this consciousness even at the hour of death
column4: one is liberated from the cycle of life and death and reaches the Supreme Abode of God.#eṣhā—such; brāhmī sthitiḥ—state of God-realization; pārtha—Arjun
column5: the son of Pritha; na—never; enām—this; prāpya—having attained; vimuhyati—is deluded; sthitvā—being established; asyām—in this; anta-kāle—at the hour of death; api—even; brahma-nirvāṇam—liberation from Maya; ṛichchhati—attains
